# Consciousness Taxonomy {#consciousness-taxonomy}

## Purpose

A structured classification of types, levels, and theories of consciousness referenced throughout the book.

---

## Part 1: Types of Consciousness

### By Philosophical Category

| Type | Definition | Key Theorist |
|------|-----------|--------------|
| Phenomenal consciousness | Subjective experience; "what it is like" | Nagel, Chalmers |
| Access consciousness | Information available for reasoning and report | Block |
| Self-consciousness | Awareness of being aware | Rosenthal |
| Proto-consciousness | Minimal, pre-reflective experience | Feinberg, Mallatt |
| Cosmic consciousness | Universe-level awareness | Cosmopsychists |

### By Biological Level

| Level | Examples | Evidence for Consciousness |
|-------|----------|---------------------------|
| Molecular | Chemical reactions, microtubules | Speculative (Orch-OR) |
| Unicellular | Bacteria, protists | Basal cognition, chemotaxis |
| Plant | Root networks, chemical signaling | Debated |
| Invertebrate | Insects, cephalopods | Behavioural, neural evidence |
| Vertebrate | Fish, birds, mammals | Strong behavioural and neural evidence |
| Primate/Human | Self-reflection, language, theory of mind | First-person reports |
| Artificial | AI, robots | Unknown/debated |

## Part 2: Levels and Dimensions

### Graded Dimensions of Consciousness

- Wakefulness: from deep sleep to full alertness
- Awareness: from no awareness to rich phenomenal experience
- Self-awareness: from no self-model to reflective self-consciousness
- Temporal depth: from immediate response to planning and memory
- Intentional complexity: from simple aboutness to nested intentionality

### States of Consciousness

| State | Wakefulness | Awareness | Self-Awareness |
|-------|-------------|-----------|----------------|
| Deep sleep | Low | Low | None |
| Dreaming | Low | Moderate | Variable |
| Waking | High | High | High |
| Flow state | High | High | Low |
| Meditation | Variable | High | Variable |
| Anaesthesia | None | None | None |
| Coma | None | None/minimal | None |
| Locked-in syndrome | High | High | High |

## Part 3: Theory Classification

### By Ontological Commitment

| Category | Theories | Core Claim |
|----------|----------|------------|
| Physicalist | GWT, RPT, AST, Emergentism | Consciousness arises from physical processes |
| Panpsychist | IIT (some readings), Russellian Monism | Consciousness is fundamental |
| Idealist | Analytic Idealism | Only consciousness exists |
| Dualist | Property Dualism | Consciousness is non-physical but depends on the physical |
| Neutral Monist | Dual-Aspect Monism | Both arise from a deeper reality |

### By Mechanism

| Mechanism Type | Theories | Key Mechanism |
|----------------|----------|---------------|
| Computational | GWT, AST | Information broadcast, attention modeling |
| Informational | IIT | Information integration |
| Biological | Biological Naturalism, Neurobiological | Specific brain processes |
| Quantum | Orch-OR, Stapp | Quantum processes |
| Thermodynamic | FEP | Free energy minimization |
| Philosophical | Panpsychism, Idealism | Ontological postulate |

[This taxonomy should be updated as the book develops]
